commands.title: abort
commands.description: Cancel the running Claude turn

commands.title: attach
commands.description: Attach to a session by name

commands.title: cancel
commands.description: Discard the message you're composing (in a chain, scraps the whole utterance)

commands.title: clear
commands.description: Start Claude fresh — keeps your history, stops re-reading it

commands.title: compress
commands.description: Compact the context into a summary Claude carries forward — keeps your history

commands.title: detach
commands.description: Leave the current session

commands.title: help
commands.description: Speak the list of commands

commands.title: job status
commands.description: Speak a one-line summary of the attached session's background jobs

commands.title: kill
commands.description: Delete a session by name

commands.title: kill job
commands.description: Kill one of the attached session's background jobs by number (from 'list jobs')

commands.title: list
commands.description: List your sessions

commands.title: list jobs
commands.description: List the attached session's detached background jobs and their status

commands.title: list models
commands.description: List the models the attached session's AI offers

commands.title: read last
commands.description: Re-read Claude's recent replies aloud

commands.title: rename
commands.description: Rename the session you're attached to

commands.title: scratch
commands.description: Toggle scratch mode: while detached, hear each transcription read back to test STT

commands.title: spawn
commands.description: Start a new session or project

commands.title: status
commands.description: What the attached session is doing

commands.title: stop
commands.description: Barge-in: stop the voice reading aloud

commands.title: summary only
commands.description: Speak only the final result of a turn; intermediate steps beep quietly instead

commands.title: usage
commands.description: Report your Claude plan's usage — session and weekly % used, with resets

commands.title: use model
commands.description: Switch the attached session's model by number (from 'list models')
